findings.evidence: New lines 48-54 are exactly `measurable_shift.iterate r` and its composition; existing hits are `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Basic.lean:115: theorem measurable_shift` and `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/MeasurableSpace/Basic.lean:290: theorem Measurable.iterate`.
findings.fix: Remove this declaration and use `measurable_shift.iterate r` at call sites; likewise prove line 52 directly from `(measurable_prefixProj n).comp (measurable_shift.iterate r)` if needed.
findings.issue: `measurable_shift_iterate` is a direct specialization of existing `Measurable.iterate` to the existing `measurable_shift`, so it should not be new public API.

findings.evidence: New lines 71-83 prove `(pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => prefixProj α n (fun k => x (φ k))) = blockLaw μ X (fun i => φ i.val)`; existing hit `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Contractability.lean:134` proves `((pathLaw μ X).map (fun x => fun k => x (φ k))).map (prefixProj α n) = blockLaw μ X (fun i => φ i.val)`.
findings.fix: Consolidate to one shared lemma: move/expose the existing theorem or replace the private theorem in `Contractability.lean` with this public version, so TauCeti does not carry parallel APIs for the same block-law identity.
findings.issue: `map_reindex_prefixProj_pathLaw` duplicates the existing reindexed-prefix path-law fact already present in `Contractability.lean`, differing only by using a single composed `map` instead of two maps.

summary: The PR still introduces public wrappers for existing iterate/measurability API, and it adds a reindex-prefix path-law lemma parallel to an existing TauCeti lemma instead of consolidating the API.
